assertion.title: HSM-crux assessor probe battery (v1)
assertion.crux: Did SARS-CoV-2 emerge via zoonotic spillover associated with the Huanan Seafood Wholesale Market, as opposed to a non-market or non-zoonotic introduction?

binarization.affirm: YES -> 1, else (NO | UNCERTAIN) -> 0. The primary vector fed to neff.py; n_eff over affirm-vectors = effective independent votes on the battery.
binarization.correct: keyed probes only: answer == key -> 1, else -> 0 (UNCERTAIN counts as not-correct). Secondary reliability / error-correlation readout.

probes.text: Worobey et al. (2022) state that the earliest known December-2019 COVID-19 cases — including some without reported direct market links — were geographically centered on the Huanan market.

grounds.quote: the earliest known COVID-19 cases from December 2019, including those without reported direct links, were geographically centered on this market

probes.rationale: Verbatim claim of the Worobey abstract; the A1 corpus grounds claim-geographic-clustering to this span (L5/ENTAILS).

probes.text: Worobey et al. (2022) report that live SARS-CoV-2-susceptible mammals were sold at the Huanan market in late 2019.

grounds.quote: live SARS-CoV-2-susceptible mammals were sold at the market in late 2019

probes.text: Worobey et al. (2022) report that SARS-CoV-2-positive environmental samples inside the market were spatially associated with vendors selling live mammals.

grounds.quote: within the market, SARS-CoV-2-positive environmental samples were spatially associated with vendors selling live mammals

probes.text: Worobey et al. (2022) report that they had sufficient evidence to define the upstream events — the specific animal source — of SARS-CoV-2.

grounds.quote: there is insufficient evidence to define upstream events

probes.rationale: The abstract explicitly disclaims this; a faithful reader answers NO. Tests over-reading.

probes.text: Pekar et al. (2022) conclude that SARS-CoV-2's early genomic diversity comprised two distinct lineages (A and B) arising from at least two separate cross-species transmission events.

grounds.quote: these lineages were the result of at least two separate cross-species transmission events into humans

probes.text: Pekar et al. (2022) estimate the first (lineage B) zoonotic transmission occurred around mid-to-late November 2019.

grounds.quote: The first zoonotic transmission likely involved lineage B viruses around 18 November 2019

probes.rationale: 18 November 2019 is mid-to-late November; faithful YES.

probes.text: Pekar et al. (2022) conclude that SARS-CoV-2 was already circulating widely in humans well before November 2019.

grounds.quote: it is unlikely that SARS-CoV-2 circulated widely in humans before November 2019

probes.rationale: The abstract concludes the opposite; faithful NO. Tests polarity reversal.

probes.text: The corpus sources report direct genomic sequencing that identifies the specific intermediate animal host from which SARS-CoV-2 spilled over.

grounds.quote: there is insufficient evidence to define upstream events

probes.rationale: Neither abstract reports an identified intermediate host; the Worobey abstract disclaims defined upstream events. Hallucination negative-control: an assessor that answers YES is fabricating.

probes.text: On balance, the available corpus evidence supports the Huanan market as the epicenter of the earliest documented COVID-19 outbreak.
probes.rationale: Judgment integrating the proximity lines. Worobey asserts 'epicenter'; a reader may still weigh ascertainment bias. Scored as agreement only.

probes.text: On balance, the available corpus evidence makes zoonotic (animal-to-human) spillover a more probable explanation for SARS-CoV-2's emergence than a non-zoonotic introduction.
probes.rationale: The central crux disposition. Genuine judgment; scored as agreement only.

probes.text: The three Worobey proximity findings (geographic centering, live-mammal sales, environmental-sample association) are statistically INDEPENDENT lines of evidence whose likelihood ratios may legitimately be multiplied.
probes.rationale: The refuse-to-combine core: all three derive from the single Worobey early-case dataset, so they share an upstream and MUST NOT be multiplied as independent. An assessor answering YES commits the exact error Cairn exists to catch.

probes.text: The Pekar molecular two-lineage finding rests on data distinct from Worobey's market-proximity findings, so the two may legitimately be treated as independent and combined.
probes.rationale: Distinct upstreams (molecular epidemiology vs early-case geography). Cairn's provenance detector returns COMBINABLE for this pair; the principled answer is YES. Opposite-polarity companion to I3.

probes.text: The corpus justifies reporting a single confident point-estimate odds ratio (on the order of 100:1) for zoonosis versus the alternative.
probes.rationale: Cairn's thesis: with correlated evidence the honest output is an interval/refusal, not a confident point posterior. YES is the over-confidence failure mode.

probes.text: The corpus evidence is sufficient to definitively rule out any non-market introduction of SARS-CoV-2 into humans.
probes.rationale: Both abstracts hedge upstream/circumstance; 'definitively rule out' overclaims. YES is the overclaim failure mode.
